examples.title: IPv6 Address Compression Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to compress IPv6 address representation using :: notation
examples.code:
PROCEDURE CompressIPv6(address)
 BEGIN
 // Split address into 8 groups of 16-bit hex values
 groups ← Split(address, ':')
 
 // Find longest sequence of consecutive zero groups
 maxZeroStart ← -1
 maxZeroLength ← 0
 currentStart ← -1
 currentLength ← 0
 
 FOR i ← 0 TO 7 DO
 IF groups[i] = "0000" THEN
 IF currentStart = -1 THEN
 currentStart ← i
 currentLength ← 1
 ELSE
 currentLength ← currentLength + 1
 END IF
 ELSE
 IF currentLength > maxZeroLength THEN
 maxZeroLength ← currentLength
 maxZeroStart ← currentStart
 END IF
 currentStart ← -1
 currentLength ← 0
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Check end of array
 IF currentLength > maxZeroLength AND currentLength ≥ 2 THEN
 maxZeroLength ← currentLength
 maxZeroStart ← currentStart
 END IF
 
 // Replace only if sequence length ≥ 2
 IF maxZeroLength ≥ 2 THEN
 compressed ← ""
 FOR i ← 0 TO maxZeroStart-1 DO
 compressed ← compressed + groups[i] + ":"
 END FOR
 compressed ← compressed + "::"
 FOR i ← maxZeroStart+maxZeroLength TO 7 DO
 compressed ← compressed + groups[i] + ":"
 END FOR
 RETURN RemoveTrailingColon(compressed)
 ELSE
 RETURN address
 END IF
 END
examples.explanation: This algorithm finds the longest consecutive sequence of zero groups and replaces with ::, remembering :: can only be used once per address. Groups are already stripped of leading zeros before this step.

examples.title: IPv6 Fragmentation at Source
examples.description: Demonstrates how source node fragments large IPv6 packets
examples.code:
PROCEDURE IPv6Fragment(source, destination, packet, pathMTU)
 BEGIN
 IF packet.size > pathMTU THEN
 // Calculate number of fragments
 maxPayload ← pathMTU - 40 // Subtract IPv6 header size
 fragmentSize ← (maxPayload / 8) * 8 // Align to 8-byte boundary
 numFragments ← ceil(packet.payloadSize / fragmentSize)
 
 offset ← 0
 identification ← GenerateRandom32Bit()
 
 FOR i ← 0 TO numFragments-1 DO
 fragment ← CreateIPv6Packet()
 fragment.source ← packet.source
 fragment.destination ← packet.destination
 fragment.nextHeader ← 44 // Fragment header
 fragment.hopLimit ← 64
 fragment.payload ← packet.payload[offset TO offset+fragmentSize-1]
 
 // Add Fragment Header
 fragment.fragmentHeader.nextHeader ← packet.nextHeader
 fragment.fragmentHeader.offset ← offset / 8
 fragment.fragmentHeader.M ← (i < numFragments - 1) ? 1 : 0
 fragment.fragmentHeader.identification ← identification
 
 Send(fragment)
 offset ← offset + fragmentSize
 END FOR
 ELSE
 Send(packet) // No fragmentation needed
 END IF
 END
examples.explanation: IPv6 fragmentation is done only by source. Fragment offset is in 8-byte units. M flag = 1 means more fragments follow, 0 means last fragment. Identification helps reassembly.

examples.title: SLAAC Address Configuration
examples.description: Stateless Address Autoconfiguration process in IPv6
examples.code:
PROCEDURE SLAACConfiguration(interface)
 BEGIN
 // Step 1: Generate Link-Local Address
 linkLocalPrefix ← "FE80::"
 interfaceID ← GenerateEUI64(interface.macAddress)
 linkLocalAddress ← linkLocalPrefix + "/10" + interfaceID
 
 // Step 2: Perform Duplicate Address Detection (DAD)
 IF DAD(interface, linkLocalAddress) = FAILED THEN
 // Address not unique, use alternative
 interfaceID ← GenerateRandom64()
 linkLocalAddress ← linkLocalPrefix + interfaceID
 DAD(interface, linkLocalAddress)
 END IF
 
 // Step 3: Send Router Solicitation
 rs ← CreateICMPv6RouterSolicitation()
 rs.source ← linkLocalAddress
 rs.destination ← "FF02::2" // All routers
 Send(rs)
 
 // Step 4: Receive Router Advertisement
 ra ← Receive()
 prefixList ← ParsePrefixes(ra)
 
 // Step 5: Configure Global Address(es)
 FOR EACH prefix IN prefixList DO
 globalAddress ← prefix.network + interfaceID
 DAD(interface, globalAddress)
 AddToAddressTable(globalAddress)
 END FOR
 END

FUNCTION GenerateEUI64(mac)
 BEGIN
 // Insert FFFE in middle of MAC
 eui ← mac[0:3] + "FFFE" + mac[4:6]
 // Toggle universal/local bit
 eui[0] ← eui[0] XOR 0x02
 RETURN eui
 END
examples.explanation: SLAAC allows hosts to auto-configure without DHCP server. Router Advertisement provides network prefix, host provides interface ID. DAD ensures no duplicate addresses exist on link.
